              -- Use the given bounds to ensure the arithmetic is correct modulo p
              rfl
            })
          <;>
          (try
            {
              -- Handle any remaining cases by contradiction or further simplification
              contradiction
            })
        }) <;>
      (try
        {
          -- Use the given bounds to ensure the arithmetic is correct modulo p
          rfl
        }) <;>
      (try
        {
          -- Handle any remaining cases by contradiction or further simplification
          contradiction
        })
  -- Use the second hypothesis to complete the proof
  exact h₂
  <;>
  (try decide)
  <;>
  (try
    {
      -- Normalize the expressions to check the congruences
      norm_num [p, Field51_as_Nat] at *
      <;>
      (try decide)
      <;>
      (try
        {
          -- Use the given bounds to ensure the arithmetic is correct modulo p
          rfl
        })
      <;>
      (try
        {
          -- Handle any remaining cases by contradiction or further simplification
          contradiction
        })
    }) <;>
  (try
    {
      -- Use the given bounds to ensure the arithmetic is correct modulo p
      rfl
    }) <;>
  (try
    {
      -- Handle any remaining cases by contradiction or further simplification
      contradiction
    })

have h₁ : as_extended q = ok (⟨q.X * q.T, q.Y * q.Z, q.Z * q.T, q.X * q.Y⟩ : EdwardsPoint) := by
    rfl
  refine' ⟨⟨q.X * q.T, q.Y * q.Z, q.Z * q.T, q.X * q.Y⟩, _⟩
  constructor
  · exact h₁
  dsimp only
  <;>
  (try decide) <;>
  (try ring_nf) <;>
  (try norm_num) <;>
  (try simp_all [Field51_as_Nat, Fin.prod_univ_five]) <;>
  (try norm_cast) <;>
  (try simp [Fin.ext_iff, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_succ]) <;>
  (try ring_nf) <;>
  (try norm_num) <;>
  (try decide) <;>
  (try aesop)

have h_main : ∃ (e : EdwardsPoint), q.as_extended = ok e ∧ (let X := Field51_as_Nat q.X; let Y := Field51_as_Nat q.Y; let Z := Field51_as_Nat q.Z; let T := Field51_as_Nat q.T; let X' := Field51_as_Nat e.X; let Y' := Field51_as_Nat e.Y; let Z' := Field51_as_Nat e.Z; let T' := Field51_as_Nat e.T; X' % p = (X * T) % p ∧ Y' % p = (Y * Z) % p ∧ Z' % p = (Z * T) % p ∧ T' % p = (X * Y) % p) := by
    use q.as_extended.get (by
      -- This is a proof that `q.as_extended` is indeed a `Ok` value, which it is by construction.
      have h : q.as_extended.isOk := by
        simp [as_extended, Result.isOk]
        <;>
        decide
      cases h with
      | intro e h =>
        simp_all [Result.isOk]
        <;>
        aesop
    )
    constructor
    · rfl
    · dsimp
      <;>
      simp_all [as_extended, mul_comm, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm]
      <;>
      norm_num
      <;>
      rfl
  
  obtain ⟨e, h₁, h₂⟩ := h_main
  refine' ⟨e, _⟩
  -- We need to show that as_extended q = ok e and the modular arithmetic conditions hold.
  constructor
  · exact h₁
  · simpa using h₂

have h₁ : as_extended q = ok (as_extended q).get := by
    cases as_extended q <;> simp_all [Result.get]
    <;> aesop
  refine' ⟨(as_extended q).get, by simpa using h₁, _⟩
  dsimp only
  <;> norm_num
  <;>
  (try decide)
  <;>
  (try ring_nf)
  <;>
  (try simp_all [p])
  <;>
  (try norm_num)
  <;>
  (try decide)

  have h₁ : Field51_as_Nat (q.X : Fin 5 → ℕ) < p := by
    have h₂ : ∀ (i : Fin 5), q.X[i]! < 2 ^ 54 := by
      intro i

      have h₄ : (q.X[i]!).val < 2 ^ 54 := h₃
